authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake. Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 25 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 50 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Topaz Lake — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake is a relatively large reservoir with 2,410 acres of surface area... created in 1922 for irrigation water storage. It straddles the Nevada-California border and is jointly managed by state wildlife agencies. The fishery features rainbow trout as the primary species, along with brown trout, cutthroat hybrids, and tiger trout.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated June 1, 2026) — CDFW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 16 years or older fishing anywhere in Lake Tahoe or Topaz Lake must possess either a California sport fishing license or a Nevada sport fishing license.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: Topaz Lake is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Trout (rainbow, brown, cutthroat hybrid, tiger trout combined).

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 5 trout; possession limit 10 trout at Topaz Lake.

parameters.counting_rule: Mountain whitefish counted separately from trout and warmwater limits.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 5 mountain whitefish; possession limit 10 mountain whitefish at Topaz Lake.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 mountain whitefish... Possession: 10 mountain whitefish...

parameters.counting_rule: Warmwater game fish (bass, bluegill, catfish, etc.) combined; of the daily 25, not more than 5 may be black bass (largemouth/smallmouth combined), and of the possession 50, not more than 10 may be black bass — see the linked black bass sub-limit rule.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 25 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 5 may be black bass); possession limit 50 (of which not more than 10 may be black bass) at Topaz Lake.
regulations.verbatimText: 25 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession:... 50 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass.

parameters.possession_condition: Counted within (not in addition to) the 25 daily / 50 possession warmwater game fish limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Of the warmwater game fish limit at Topaz Lake, not more than 5 per day (10 in possession) may be black bass (largemouth or smallmouth).
regulations.verbatimText: not more than 5 may be black bass... not more than 10 may be black bass

parameters.note: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours at Topaz Lake run from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: Topaz Lake straddles the CA/NV border; anglers 16+ must hold either a California sport fishing license or a Nevada fishing license, honored anywhere on the lake by reciprocity.

regulations.citation: 2026 CA Freshwater Sport Fishing Regs — General Information / License Provisions (Lake Tahoe & Topaz Lake)
regulations.humanSummary: Anglers 16+ fishing anywhere on Topaz Lake must hold a California sport fishing license or a Nevada fishing license.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 16 years or older fishing anywhere in Lake Tahoe or Topaz Lake must possess either a California sport fishing license or a Nevada sport fishing license.

reciprocity.note: California recognizes a valid Nevada sport fishing license anywhere on Topaz Lake, since the lake straddles the state line.
Nevada recognizes a valid California sport fishing license anywhere on Topaz Lake, since the lake straddles the state line.
